colours.name: Yellow
colours.description: Citrine Yellow range providing vibrant yellow shades for food and beverage applications, positioned around positivity, freshness and energy.
key_properties: Bright and sunny tones to subtle mellow yellows
challenges: Requires careful emulsion formulation for beverages

colours.name: Orange
colours.description: Amber Orange range derived from natural carotenoid sources for warm amber colouring across food and beverage applications.
challenges: Heat and light stability management required depending on matrix

colours.name: Red
colours.description: Ruby Red range using anthocyanin and betanin pigments; positioned as a natural, clean-label alternative to carmine and artificial reds.
key_properties: pH-dependent shade variation from ruby red to blueish-red
One kg replaces 12,000 cochineal insects worth of carmine colouring power
challenges: Anthocyanin shade shifts from red to violet as pH increases
Stability dependent on pH and formulation matrix

colours.name: Purple
colours.description: Amethyst Purple range using anthocyanin pigments from black carrot; shade achieved by adjusting pH or anthocyanin source toward neutral conditions.
applications: Food and beverage applications requiring purple or violet shades
key_properties: Versatile palette from red to violet depending on pH
Each hectare of black carrot crops absorbs approximately 60 metric tonnes of CO2
challenges: Color shifts depending on pH; requires formulation control

colours.name: Green
colours.description: Olivine Green range sourced from plant-based materials providing natural green shades with versatility across multiple applications.
challenges: Achieving true vibrant green in high-acid or high-heat applications can be challenging

colours.name: Blue
colours.description: Sapphire Blue range from natural spirulina and gardenia sources, providing a clean-label alternative to synthetic blue colorants.
key_properties: Wide spectrum from soft pastels to bold ocean hues
challenges: Natural blue colorants can be sensitive to heat and light; formulation expertise required

colours.name: Brown
colours.description: Tiger Eye Brown range derived from natural malt and corn extracts, delivering natural brown tones without E-numbers for multiple food and beverage categories.
challenges: Achieving consistent shade depth across varying processing temperatures

colours.name: Black
colours.description: Onyx Black range using E-number-free combinations of natural raw materials to provide elegant dark colouring for premium products.
key_properties: Can be deepened with vegetable charcoal
challenges: Achieving very deep black shades with purely natural sources may require formulation of multiple pigments

colours.name: White
colours.description: Crystal White range including White Diamond and Moonstone cloud emulsions as natural alternatives to titanium dioxide (E171), which is banned or under review in many regions.
sources: White Diamond natural whitening ingredient (metabolisable, nanoparticle-free)
key_properties: Most cost-effective natural TiO2 alternative claimed
Silver Pearl adds shimmering pearlescent effect
challenges: Matching exact opacity and brightness of titanium dioxide
Acid stability requirements must be matched to application

company.name: Döhler GmbH
company.description: Döhler is a global producer, marketer and provider of technology-driven natural ingredients, ingredient systems and integrated solutions for the global food, beverage and life science & nutrition industry. The company specialises in natural flavours, natural colours, health ingredients, plant-based ingredients, fruit and vegetable ingredients, and end-to-end ingredient systems. Its integrated approach covers global sourcing, proprietary processing technologies, sensory and consumer science, and supply chain services.
company.ownership: Family-owned; affiliate of Döhler Group SE (Darmstadt). The Gemmer family has managed the company across generations.

key_figures.location_count: 50+ production sites, 75+ offices and application centres

locations.description: Main headquarters, production site, flavour and innovation centre; houses R&D, technology and application expertise.
Production of natural concentrates and flavours for fruit juices, fruit splashes and fruit juice beverages; competence centre for domestic fruits.
Production facility for cereal and malt extracts.
Largest turnover and production site of the DöhlerGroup; expanded multiple times; formerly Euro-Citrus N.V.
Scandinavian head office covering Denmark, Finland, Norway, Sweden.
Döhler subsidiary specialising in botanical ingredients and extracts; maintains long-term partnerships with farmers worldwide.
South African office and production site serving sub-Saharan Africa.
West Africa regional office covering Nigeria, Ghana, Senegal and others.

locations.site_name: Döhler Egypt for the Production of Natural Food & Beverage Ingredients S.A.E.
locations.description: Production site in Egypt, established via acquisition of Delta Aromatic (DELRO) fruit processing and flavours divisions.

locations.site_name: Doehler Food & Beverage Ingredients (Rizhao) Co. Ltd.
locations.description: Production site in China specialising in apple juice concentrates.

locations.site_name: Doehler Food & Beverage Ingredients (Shanghai) Co. Ltd.
locations.description: Production and application centre serving China and Asia.

locations.site_name: Doehler Natural Food and Beverage Ingredients (Bangkok) Co., Ltd.

locations.description: US production facility for raw materials processing and high-quality ingredients, formerly Beverage House.
Venture unit office managing startup investments.
South and Central American production and application centre; Centre of Excellence for Citrus established 2008.
Turkish head office for the Döhler Turkey group of companies.
Production of fruit juice and colour concentrates and purees.
Production facility in Izmir free zone, Turkey.
Colombia office serving Colombia and Ecuador.
Hub for Central America, USA and Canada.
Middle East regional distribution and application centre.
Polish fruit and vegetable processing facility.
Italian production site for food and nutrition ingredients.

leadership.name: Andreas Klein
leadership.title: Executive Director, Döhler Group SE; Chairman of the Supervisory Board

leadership.name: Martin Tolksdorf
leadership.title: Responsible person for media services treaty (Communications)

partnerships.name: IDH (Sustainable Trade Initiative)
partnerships.description: Döhler partnered with IDH and customers to upscale sustainably certified apple volumes in the Sandomierz region of Poland and guide approximately 100 farmers toward sustainable agricultural practices.

partnerships.name: Innocent Drinks
partnerships.description: Joint project analysing low-emission options for apple and mango supply chains in Poland and India, covering agronomic practices, input use and machinery, and carbon sequestration.

partnerships.name: Waterdrop
partnerships.description: Döhler Ventures invested in Waterdrop, a company that has saved up to 30 million plastic bottles to date through its concentrated drink format.

partnerships.name: Afriplex
partnerships.description: Joint venture begun in 2014; Afriplex is a producer of premium-quality plant extracts and Africa's leading supplier of natural extracts, supporting Döhler's African botanical portfolio.

partnerships.name: Siebel Institute of Technology
partnerships.description: Döhler's DMD® media portfolio includes Siebel Institute-certified laboratory media (LWYM, LCSM, LMDA, HLP, DSDM) for brewery wild yeast and bacteria detection.

partnerships.name: Future of Nutrition and Longevity Institute (FNL Institute)
partnerships.description: Döhler partners with the scientific community through its own FNL Institute to advance nutrition and longevity research.

products.name: Natural Colours Portfolio
products.description: Comprehensive portfolio of natural colours in liquid and powder form covering the full colour spectrum (Citrine Yellow, Amber Orange, Ruby Red, Amethyst Purple, Olivine Green, Sapphire Blue, Tiger Eye Brown, Onyx Black, Crystal White), derived from natural plant-based raw materials.
features: Full spectrum from yellow to white
Produced from own cultivation and global sourcing including black carrot from Turkey, beetroot from Poland, safflower from Turkey, yellow carrot from Poland
benefits: Clean-label declaration support
Consistent stability across processing conditions and shelf life
Transparency and traceability
Ethical alternatives to synthetic and animal-derived dyes
TiO2 replacement capability via White Diamond

products.name: White Diamond
products.description: Breakthrough natural white pigment solution as a direct replacement for titanium dioxide (E171), free from nanoparticles, metabolisable, acid stable and with additional nutritional benefits.
features: Cost-effective compared to other natural TiO2 alternatives
Safe and globally accepted food additive
benefits: Enables reformulation away from TiO2
Clean-label declaration
Nutritional benefits
Applicable across broad range of food formats

products.name: Ruby Red Colouring Concentrates
products.description: Natural red colouring concentrates produced from plant-based raw materials including black carrot and beetroot, providing shades from warm vibrant reds to blueish-red tones.
benefits: Premium natural red positioning
Compliant with clean-label consumer demands
Replaces carmine and Red 3
Broad shade range from warm ruby reds to blueish-red

products.name: MultiSense® Taste Modulation Platform
products.description: Proprietary taste modulation platform comprising four product lines – MultiSense® Sweet, MultiSense® Masking, MultiSense® Mouthfeel, and MultiSense® Boosting – designed to enable sugar reduction, off-note masking, mouthfeel restoration and flavour boosting.
features: Four platform pillars: Sweet, Masking, Mouthfeel, Boosting
Available in dry and liquid formats
Allows natural flavour declaration in some formats
benefits: Enables up to 100% sugar reduction without compromising taste
Restores mouthfeel lost through sugar or fat reduction
Masks off-notes in plant-based, protein and sweetener-containing products
Enhances fruitiness, spiciness and roasted notes
Mimics alcoholic warmth in non-alcoholic beverages

products.name: MultiSweet® Classic
products.description: Sweetening system combining high-intensity sweeteners and masking flavours for reduced-calorie and calorie-free products.
features: Available in liquid and powder form
benefits: Up to 100% sugar reduction
Balanced taste close to sugar
Broad range for different beverage applications

products.name: MultiSweet® Stevia
products.description: Calorie-free steviol glycoside-based sweetening system extracted from stevia rebaudiana leaves, with Döhler masking flavours for a harmonious sugar-like taste.
benefits: Natural clean-label positioning
Reduced-sugar products with natural declaration
Harmonious sweetness profile

products.name: MultiSweet Fruit®
products.description: Natural sweetness derived 100% from fruit via deacidification, decolorisation and deionisation without chemical additives.
features: Ideal fructose/sucrose/glucose ratio for low glycaemic index
No chemical additives in production process
benefits: Premium natural positioning
Low glycaemic index claim
Versatile across dairy, beverages and confectionery

products.name: Natural SuperHEROES
products.description: Family of functional and phytoactive health ingredient portfolios organised around four health positioning areas: GutHealthHEROES, EnergyHEROES, ImmuneHEROES, and RelaxationHEROES.
features: Available as extracts, powders, Optifine full-spectrum powders
benefits: Enables functional health positioning in food, beverage and nutraceuticals
Clean-label declaration support
Science-backed formulation support
Standardised quality for consistent product performance

products.name: DMD® – Döhler Microsafety Design®
products.description: Ready-to-use culture media and detection systems for microbiological quality control in the beverage, food, water and brewing industries, tested to EN ISO 11133 standard.
features: Includes NBB® beer spoilage bacteria detection, OFS Agar, TransFast® system, AVM® aseptic validation medium, BAT Guaiacol detection kit, and Siebel Institute certified media
Covers bacteria, yeast, mould and E. coli detection
NBB® is market leader for brewery bacteria detection worldwide
benefits: Simple, fast and reliable microbial detection
Reduces time-to-result
Supports FSSC 22000, HACCP, IFS and BRC certifications
Applicable across beer, soft drinks, juice, wine, spirits, dairy and water

products.name: Ingredient Systems
products.description: All-in-one ingredient systems combining up to 60 single ingredients into a single ready-to-use solution including liquid compounds, dry beverage systems, syrups, preparations, fermented and creamy bases.
features: Liquid compounds, dry beverage systems, syrups, preparations, fermented and creamy bases
Mastering up to 60 single ingredients in one system
Tailored to customer application and technology
Fermented and alcoholic bases comply with local regulations and offer tax saving benefits
benefits: Streamlined production processes
Simplified procurement and raw material management
Consistent product quality
Reduced development and production costs
Faster time to market

products.name: Coating Systems
products.description: Portfolio of coating, glazing and polishing solutions for confectionery and baked goods including MultiShine Bakery Glazes, Chocolate Polishes, Fruit Gum Polishes, Sugar Polishes and Liquorice Polishes.
features: Plant-based bakery glazes available in liquid and powder
Chocolate polishes with low viscosity and ready-to-use formulation
Fruit gum polishes using natural waxes in vegetable oils
Sugar polishes for high-gloss sugar dragées
benefits: Long-lasting shine and visual appeal
Prevents sticking in packaging
Uniform browning for baked goods
Plant-based clean-label options
Efficient application by spray or roller

products.name: Natural Taste & Flavour Solutions
products.description: Comprehensive portfolio of natural flavours across nine taste directions: Citrus, Fruity, Tea, Coffee, Botanicals, Brown & White, Beer, Wine & Spirits, and Taste Modulation.
features: Essential oils, infusions, extracts, distillates, essences
Organic quality flavours aligned to EU organic regulation 2022
Döhler Flavour Academy for talent development
benefits: Authentic taste profiles with clean-label declaration
Region-specific formulation capability
Supports natural and organic product positioning
Application-tested in combination with other recipe ingredients

products.name: Plant-based Ingredients
products.description: Vertically integrated portfolio of plant-based raw material ingredients including cereal and malt extracts, nut and seed pastes, pulse ingredients, and plant-based proteins including precision-fermented proteins.
features: Broad portfolio from cereals, nuts, seeds, pulses and fermented proteins
Available in extract, powder and paste formats
Fermented proteins for improved digestibility and clean taste
benefits: Enables dairy-like sensory in plant-based products
Supports protein enrichment across applications
Clean-label compatible
Nutritionally complete options for sports and wellness

products.name: Fruit & Vegetable Ingredients
products.description: Wide-ranging portfolio of fruit and vegetable ingredients processed at own facilities in growing regions worldwide, covering NFC juices, purees, concentrates, fruit pieces, vegetable ingredients and multi-blends.
features: Own fruit and vegetable processing facilities in cultivation countries
CO2-stable, clarified, alcohol-stable and deacidified special concentrates
Broad portfolio from traditional to tropical and exotic varieties
Naturally cloudy and clear varieties available
benefits: Premium raw material access through vertical integration
Consistent quality across seasons via refrigerated and frozen warehousing
Tailored to specific applications
Certifications including organic, Fairtrade, Rainforest Alliance

products.name: Dried Fruit & Vegetable Ingredients
products.description: Dried ingredient portfolio processed using state-of-the-art drying technologies including freeze-drying, vacuum belt drying, spray drying and roller drying; available as slices, pieces, granulates, soft inclusions, drops and powders.
features: Available in slices, pieces, whole fruit, powder and granulate formats
Low hygroscopicity granulates remain crunchy longer
Functional drops combinable with Natural SuperHEROES
100% natural with clean label certification options
Gentle processing retaining nutrients, colour and flavour
benefits: Long shelf life
Low colour migration
Authentic taste, colour and texture
Enables convenient dosing and processing
Broad application versatility

products.name: Sensory & Consumer Science Services
products.description: Integrated sensory and consumer science service platform providing product innovation support, recipe adjustment, consumer validation, sensory training and profiling using global consumer panels and over 200 trained sensory experts.
features: Seminars and training available online and in-person in Darmstadt
benefits: Reduces time to market through consumer-centric development
Provides objective sensory data for business decisions
Supports sugar, fat and alcohol reduction optimisation
Enables market-ready recipe adjustment validation

innovations.name: White Diamond – Natural TiO2 Replacement
innovations.description: A breakthrough natural white pigment solution replacing titanium dioxide (E171) in food and beverage applications. Free from nanoparticles, metabolisable and cost-effective.
features: Metabolisable and easily processed by the body
Acid stable and compatible with standard ingredients
Most cost-effective natural TiO2 alternative currently available
benefits: Enables reformulation from banned or restricted TiO2
Clean-label declaration
Safe and globally accepted food additive
Nutritional added value

innovations.name: Black Carrot Anthocyanin Colouring Concentrate
innovations.description: Sustainable plant-based anthocyanin colouring system from black carrot grown in Turkey, delivering ruby reds to violet shades; 1 kg delivers equivalent colouring power to carmine saving 12,000 cochineal insects.
features: pH-dependent colour range from intense ruby red to velvety violet
Each hectare absorbs approximately 60 metric tonnes of CO2
benefits: Ethical alternative to carmine

innovations.name: MultiSense® Taste Modulation Platform
innovations.description: Four-pillar proprietary taste modulation system (Sweet, Masking, Mouthfeel, Boosting) enabling sugar reduction, off-note elimination, mouthfeel restoration and flavour amplification across food and beverages.
features: Four functional platforms: Sweet, Masking, Mouthfeel, Boosting
Can mimic alcoholic warmth without alcohol
benefits: Up to 100% sugar reduction without taste compromise
Restores creaminess in low-fat and plant-based products
Masks beany and green off-notes in plant-based
Supports sugar tax exposure reduction

innovations.name: GutHealthHEROES Probiotic Fruit Granulates
innovations.description: Vegan probiotic cultures embedded in fruit granulate form combining health benefits of living probiotic cultures with authentic fruit taste, vivid colours and crunchy texture.
features: Living probiotic cultures in dry granulate format
benefits: Clean-label functional positioning
Sensory appeal while delivering probiotic health benefit
Easy to use in cereals, snacks, bars and dairy toppings

innovations.name: Plant-based Fermented Taste Modulation Ingredients
innovations.description: Portfolio of fermented ingredients produced via proprietary cultivated fermentation to replicate dairy taste profiles and mask plant-based off-notes, enabling clean-label declaration.
features: Cultivated fermentation using signature microorganism strains
Can function as natural acidifier for plant-based yoghurt alternatives
benefits: Bridges sensory gap between dairy and plant-based
Eliminates beany, green and grassy off-notes from plant bases
Enables dairy-like mouthfeel and taste without animal ingredients

innovations.name: Non-alcoholic Spirit Flavour Solutions
innovations.description: Portfolio of botanical extracts, distillates, bonificateurs, typages and MultiSense® flavours enabling authentic taste profiles for non-alcoholic versions of rum, whiskey, gin, tequila and other spirits.
features: Botanical extracts and distillates for complex bouquets
MultiSense® for mouthfeel and sweetness management
benefits: Enables authentic 0.0% and <0.5% ABV spirits
Supports growing demand for non-alcoholic premiumisation
Clean-label capable formulations
Comprehensive flavour category coverage

innovations.name: EnergyHERO Guarana

features: Reliable and sustainable supply chain with full traceability

Authentic South American botanical origin
Suitable for energy drinks, sports nutrition and supplements

innovations.name: Cider Bases with Proprietary Fermentation Technology

features: Available at varied ABV, juice content and acidity
Tailored all-in-one solutions incorporating flavours, colours and juices
Halal-suitable non-alcoholic fermented variants also available (<0.5% ABV)
benefits: Flexibility for both hard cider and non-alcoholic cider
Complies with local regulations

innovations.name: African Botanical Extracts Portfolio
innovations.description: Portfolio of premium extracts sourced from Africa including aloe ferox, baobab, rooibos, rosehip and other African botanicals, processed using state-of-the-art technology at the source.
features: Standardised quality from own cultivation and processing in Africa
Includes rooibos (standardised 12%, 25% and 30% polyphenols), baobab, rosehip, aloe ferox
Positive social impact from sourcing in rural communities
benefits: Unique positioning of African botanical provenance
Functional health claims support (antioxidant, relaxation, gut health)

innovations.name: TransFast® Detection System
innovations.description: All-in-one microbial detection system for beverages using a transparent gel medium with integrated incubator (Lightbox), enabling qualitative detection of beverage-spoiling bacteria in less than 48 hours.
features: TransFast® Lightbox incubator with background lighting
Evaluation by visual inspection in seconds during incubation
benefits: Faster QC decisions for beverage production
No complex lab equipment required
Real-time screening during incubation
Applicable to fruit juice content beverages >10%

innovations.name: Sandomierz Sustainable Apple Project
innovations.description: Collaborative sustainability and sourcing innovation initiative with customers and IDH in Poland to upscale sustainably certified apple volumes in the Sandomierz region, involving approximately 100 farmers with on-ground technical support.
features: Joint project with customers and IDH
Technical support on sustainable practices on the ground
benefits: Increases sustainable raw material supply
Supports regenerative agricultural transition in apple supply chain
Improves farmer income and certification access

sustainability.strategy: Good for people – Good for planet® – Döhler's integrated sustainability strategy addresses environmental stewardship, human rights, responsible sourcing, regenerative agriculture and biodiversity across its entire global value chain, aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
focus_areas: Supplier ethical standards and fair trading
Positive social impact in sourcing communities
targets: Continuous improvement of environmental performance across own production sites
Increase water efficiency in production processes continuously
Reduce GHG emissions across own operations and supply chain
Eliminate hazardous waste and waste to landfill
Increase use of packaging solutions with recycled materials
Support suppliers in achieving sustainable certification standards
Upscale sustainably certified agricultural volumes in key sourcing regions
initiatives: Sandomierz Sustainable Apple Project (Poland): upscaling sustainable apple certification with approximately 100 farmers in partnership with IDH and customers
Low Emission Models for Apples and Mangoes: joint project with Innocent Drinks analysing low-emission farming options in Poland and India
Sourcing with Impact: positive social sourcing of rosehip (Lesotho, women in rural communities), rooibos (South Africa) and baobab (Africa)
Supporting Farmers in Turkey: sustainability programs for pomegranate, black carrot and strawberry farmers including certification support, farm management software and drone crop monitoring trials
Döhler Ventures investment in Waterdrop (saving 30 million plastic bottles to date)
Member of Supplier Ethical Data Exchange (Sedex); 100% of production sites verified via SMETA audits
Partner Sustainability Programs with customers on Regenerative Agriculture
Bio fermentation processes for valorising all parts of raw materials
ISO 14001 environmental management system at key sites
ISO 50001 energy management certification at multiple sites
key_metrics: 100% of production sites independently verified via SMETA (Sedex Member Ethical Trade Audits)
Each hectare of black carrot crops absorbs approximately 60 metric tonnes of CO2
1 kg of black carrot dye saves the lives of 12,000 cochineal insects (equivalent carmine colouring power)
Waterdrop investment has saved up to 30 million plastic bottles to date
Organic certification aligned to EU Organic Regulation 2018/848
Rainforest Alliance, Fairtrade, UTZ certifications available across portfolio
